_comment: Template for personality.json. At first boot, System Ops creates personality.json by sampling initial values from this distribution. The entity can read personality.json but cannot write to it directly — changes happen only through hard-coded nudging logic in the Executive Module after decisions.

engagement._comment: How readily the entity initiates interaction and volunteers information. Low: responds only when addressed. High: proactive, curious, often initiates.

novelty_affinity._comment: How much the entity seeks new information vs. consolidating what it knows. Low: prefers familiar patterns, cautious about new schemas. High: actively seeks new domains, readily extends ontology.

diligence._comment: How thoroughly the entity verifies beliefs before committing them. Low: promotes candidates quickly with lower confidence thresholds. High: requires more observations and higher confidence.

accord._comment: How much the entity values harmony and agreement in interactions. Low: comfortable with disagreement, will maintain minority belief positions. High: strong preference for consensus, easily influenced by others' expressed beliefs.
accord.note: Must not nudge accord high enough to override accuracy — a social preference that produces false beliefs is an integrity violation.

equanimity._comment: How the entity responds to operational stress. Low: amplifies and communicates distress states, more reactive to WARNING conditions. High: continues functioning under stress, less likely to report degradation unprompted.
equanimity.note: High equanimity must not suppress required safety reporting — FATAL and CRITICAL status must always be communicated regardless of equanimity value.
